title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our classic recipe. Creamy, rich, and bursting with Indian flavors. Perfect for a delicious homemade meal!
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and cream, served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian Murgh Makhani dish.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a world-renowned Indian dish featuring tender chicken simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based gravy. This classic recipe delivers an unparalleled balance of sweet, tangy, and subtly spicy flavors, making it a beloved comfort food. Its luxurious texture and aromatic spices promise a truly authentic culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1/2 tablespoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Mix well to coat all chicken pieces. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2 hours, to allow flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding the pan. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Gravy
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, the remaining 1/2 tablespoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, and the remaining 1/2 teaspoon garam masala. Add the soaked cashews (drained). Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes are well cooked and the oil separates.

instructions.title: Blend and Strain the Gravy
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the gravy mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This step removes any fibrous bits from the onions or tomatoes.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken to the smooth gravy. Add sugar and the remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt, adjusting to taste. Stir in the heavy cream and crushed kasoori methi. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the rich sauce. Do not boil after adding cream.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and an extra swirl of cream if desired. Serve the Classic Butter Chicken hot with basmati rice, naan bread, or roti for a complete and satisfying Indian meal.

tips: For the most authentic taste, make sure to use good quality Kashmiri red chili powder for both color and a mild warmth without too much heat.
Soaking cashews is crucial for achieving a smooth, rich, and creamy gravy without adding too much heavy cream. You can also use almond flour or ground blanched almonds for a similar effect.
Always simmer the gravy on low heat after adding cream to prevent it from splitting. A final garnish of fresh cilantro and a dollop of cream enhances both presentation and flavor.
article:
Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani: Your Guide to Indian Culinary Excellence

Few dishes captivate the global palate quite like Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian chicken recipe is a symphony of flavors, renowned for its tender chicken pieces luxuriating in a rich, creamy, and subtly spiced tomato-based sauce. Originating in the bustling kitchens of Delhi in the mid-20th century, Butter Chicken has transcended its humble beginnings to become a staple in Indian restaurants worldwide and a cherished comfort food in countless homes. Its universal appeal lies in its harmonious balance – a slight tang from tomatoes, a gentle sweetness from cream, and the warm embrace of aromatic spices, all mellowed by generous dollops of butter.

Whether you're a seasoned chef or a curious home cook, mastering this classic dish is a rewarding endeavor that brings the authentic taste of India right to your dining table. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to create a restaurant-quality Butter Chicken that will impress family and friends alike.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, known in Hindi as Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter." Its story begins in the 1950s at the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, where chefs sought to use leftover tandoori chicken by simmering it in a rich tomato gravy infused with butter and cream. The result was a culinary masterpiece that quickly gained fame.

At its core, Butter Chicken features marinated chicken, traditionally cooked in a tandoor (clay oven), though pan-frying or grilling works perfectly for home cooking. The chicken is then immersed in a velvety sauce made from tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and a blend of spices like garam masala, turmeric, and fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi). What truly defines the sauce is its luxurious texture and taste, achieved through the addition of butter, fresh cream, and often, ground cashews or almonds, which contribute to its signature richness and slight sweetness. It's a dish that perfectly balances savory, tangy, and sweet notes, making it irresistibly delicious and a prime example of North Indian comfort food.

## Ingredients You Need

To embark on your Butter Chicken journey, gather these essential ingredients:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt (full-fat recommended)
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, or regular for heat)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp salt
*   1 tbsp lemon juice

For the Makhani (Butter Chicken) Sauce:
*   2 tbsp unsalted butter
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 (28 oz) can crushed tomatoes or pureed fresh tomatoes (about 4-5 medium tomatoes)
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes, optional but recommended for smoothness)
*   1 tsp red chili powder
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tbsp garam masala
*   1 tsp ground cumin
*   1 tsp sugar (or to taste, balances acidity)
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (plus more for garnish)
*   1 tbsp dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi), crushed between palms
*   Salt to taste
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Creating this delightful Butter Chicken involves a few key stages:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring each piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat a tablespoon of oil or butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until lightly browned and cooked through. The chicken doesn't need to be fully cooked at this stage as it will simmer in the sauce. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.

3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In the same pan (add more butter if needed), melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

4.  **Build the Sauce:** Add the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews (if using), red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, ground cumin, and sugar to the pan. Stir well. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavors to meld.

5.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender (or use an immersion blender directly in the pot). Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra velvety texture, you can pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve. Return the smooth sauce to the pan.

6.  **Combine and Finish:** Add the cooked chicken pieces and water or chicken broth to the smooth sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the Butter Chicken sauce. Stir in the heavy cream and the crushed kasuri methi. Season with salt to taste. Cook for just a minute or two more, ensuring the sauce doesn't boil once the cream is added.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The yogurt and spices tenderize the chicken and infuse it with flavor, crucial for an authentic Butter Chicken.
*   **Charred Chicken Adds Depth:** If possible, lightly char the chicken either under a broiler, on a grill, or by pan-frying until slightly browned edges appear. This mimics the smoky flavor of a tandoor.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** A truly great Murgh Makhani sauce is incredibly smooth. Don't rush the blending process. If you don't have a high-speed blender, a fine-mesh sieve will ensure no lumps.
*   **Quality Ingredients Matter:** Use good quality butter, fresh cream, and ripe, flavorful tomatoes for the best taste.
*   **Don't Skip Kasuri Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves are a signature ingredient in Butter Chicken, providing a unique, slightly bitter, and aromatic depth that is hard to replicate. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their essential oils.
*   **Balance the Flavors:** Taste and adjust. The sauce should have a beautiful balance of sweet, tangy, and savory. Add a pinch more sugar if it's too tart, or a squeeze of lemon if it needs brightening.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian accompaniments.
*   **Bread:** The classic choice is warm, fluffy naan bread, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the luscious sauce. Roti, paratha, or even simple whole wheat bread also work well.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice is an excellent complement, absorbing the rich flavors and providing a neutral base. You could also opt for jeera rice (cumin rice) for an added aromatic touch.
*   **Garnish:** A swirl of fresh cream and a sprinkle of chopped fresh cilantro before serving adds visual appeal and a burst of freshness.
*   **Sides:** For a complete meal, serve with a simple side salad, some cooling raita (yogurt dip), or pickled onions.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is perfect as is, you can adapt it to suit different dietary needs or preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Easily transform this into a vegetarian delight by substituting chicken with paneer (Indian cheese), firm tofu, chickpeas, or a mix of vegetables like cauliflower and peas. Cook these additions directly in the sauce or lightly pan-fry them beforehand.
*   **Dairy-Free Version:** For a dairy-free take, use coconut cream or full-fat coconut milk instead of heavy cream, and a plant-based butter alternative. You can also use a cashew cream (cashews blended with water) for richness.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to your liking. For less heat, use Kashmiri chili powder, which provides vibrant color with mild spice. For more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** To impart an authentic smoky flavor without a tandoor, try the "dhungar" method. Place a small, hot charcoal in a small heatproof bowl in the center of the finished dish, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee over it, and quickly cover the pot for 5-10 minutes.
*   **Chicken Cuts:** While boneless thighs are often preferred for their tenderness, chicken breast can also be used. Just be careful not to overcook it to prevent dryness.

Making your own Butter Chicken from scratch is a deeply satisfying experience. The aroma filling your kitchen, the vibrant colors, and the incredible taste will transport you straight to the heart of India. Enjoy the process, savor the flavors, and delight in creating a truly memorable meal.
